Property 'Button.variant' had its type changed (property): Return type of `variant` changed from `'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'` to `'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'`

  propValue: plain

Fix strategy:
Strategy: PropTypeChange
Component: Button
Prop: variant
From: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'
To: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'

Rule [family:Select]:
## Select Family Migration

Target structure (correct v6 composition):
```jsx

    <SelectList isAriaMultiselectable={...}>

        <SelectList />
        <Select />
      </SelectOption>
    </SelectList>
    <SelectGroup innerRef={...} label={...} labelHeadingLevel={...} titleId={...} />
  </Select>
```

### Fix 3
Line: 250
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-button]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <Button> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <Button> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <Button variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></Button>
  After:  <Button variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <Button> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <Button><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</Button>
  After:  <Button icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</Button>

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: TextInputGroupUtilities

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: Button
Prop: icon

```
  251                <TimesIcon aria-hidden />

```

### Fix 4
Line: 224
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-menutoggle]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <MenuToggle> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <MenuToggle> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <MenuToggle variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></MenuToggle>
  After:  <MenuToggle variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <MenuToggle> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <MenuToggle><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</MenuToggle>
  After:  <MenuToggle icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</MenuToggle>

Incident context:

  module: @patternfly/react-core

### Fix 5

Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-textinputgroupmain]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <TextInputGroupMain> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <TextInputGroupMain> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <TextInputGroupMain variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></TextInputGroupMain>
  After:  <TextInputGroupMain variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <TextInputGroupMain> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <TextInputGroupMain><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</TextInputGroupMain>
  After:  <TextInputGroupMain icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</TextInputGroupMain>

### Fix 6
Line: 3
Rule [sd-test-select-transitive-behavioral-changes]:
Select is affected by behavioral changes in rendered sub-components:
- Select renders Popper which has a behavioral change: Default value for 'appendTo' prop on Popper changed from 'inline' to () => document.body

Tests for Select may need updating. Fix options depend on the specific change:
- For portal changes: use waitFor(), popperProps=, or within(document.body)
- For role/DOM changes: update getByRole() queries to match new roles
- For attribute changes: update querySelector/getAttribute calls

Incident context:
  importedName: Select
  localName: Select
  module: @patternfly/react-core

Code context:
```
    2  import {
    3    Select,
    4    SelectOption,

```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/distributed-tracing-console-plugin/source/web/src/components/TypeaheadSelect.tsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
I'll start by reading the file to understand the current code.

Now let me analyze each fix:

**Fix 1** (Line 250): `Button variant="plain"` — type changed to add `'stateful'`. The current value `"plain"` is still valid in the new type. **No code change needed.**

**Fix 2** (Line 264): Select family migration. Two sub-incidents:
- Line 269: `shouldFocusFirstItemOnOpen={false}` — This prop still exists on Select in v6 (it's listed in "Props that stay on <Select>"). The incident references `focusTimeoutDelay` but the actual prop used is `shouldFocusFirstItemOnOpen` which is still valid. **No code change needed for this prop.**
- Line 264: `onSelect` type changed from `value?: string | number` to `value?: SelectOptionProps['value']`. The current code has `value: string | number | undefined`. Need to update the type signature to match the new API.

**Fix 3** (Line 250): Button icon children → `icon` prop. The Button contains only `<TimesIcon aria-hidden />`. Move to `icon` prop.

**Fix 4** (Line 224): MenuToggle icon children → `icon` prop. Looking at the MenuToggle (lines 224-256), it contains a `<TextInputGroup>` as children, not an icon. The children are a text input group, not an icon. **No icon to move — this is a false positive based on the rule description. The MenuToggle has `variant="typeahead"` with TextInputGroup children, not icon children.**

**Fix 5** (Line 233): TextInputGroupMain icon children → `icon` prop. Looking at TextInputGroupMain (lines 233-246), it's a self-closing-ish element with props only, no icon children. **No icon children to move.**

**Fix 6** (Line 3): Select behavioral change (Popper appendTo default changed). This is an informational/test-related fix. No companion test files listed. The code doesn't set `popperProps` on Select. **No code change needed for this — it's advisory.**

Let me re-examine Fix 2 more carefully. The `onSelect` callback type changed:

Current code (line 114-122):
```typescript
const onSelect = (
    _event: React.MouseEvent<Element, MouseEvent> | undefined,
    value: string | number | undefined,
  ) => {
```

The new type is `value?: SelectOptionProps['value']`. Since `SelectOptionProps` is already imported, I should update the type signature.

Let me also re-examine Fix 4 more carefully - the MenuToggle at line 224 has `variant="typeahead"` and its children is a `<TextInputGroup>`. There's no icon element as a direct child. The rule says to move icon content to `icon` prop, but there's no icon content here. However, there IS an icon inside the children (the `TimesIcon` inside the Button inside TextInputGroupUtilities inside TextInputGroup inside MenuToggle). That's deeply nested and not a direct icon child of MenuToggle. **No change needed for Fix 4.**

Now for Fix 5: TextInputGroupMain at lines 233-246 has no icon children — it's configured with props only and is self-closing within the JSX structure. **No change needed.**

Let me apply the changes:

1. **Fix 2**: Update `onSelect` type signature to use `SelectOptionProps['value']`
2. **Fix 3**: Move `<TimesIcon aria-hidden />` from Button children to `icon` prop

arguments.after: <Button variant="plain" onClick={onClearButtonClick} aria-label="Clear input value" icon={<TimesIcon aria-hidden />} />
arguments.before:
<Button variant="plain" onClick={onClearButtonClick} aria-label="Clear input value">
              <TimesIcon aria-hidden />
            </Button>

content.text: Now let me verify the final file looks correct:

The file looks correct. All changes have been applied properly. Let me verify that all imports are still valid and nothing is unused:

- `TimesIcon` — still used in the `icon` prop on line 250 ✓
- `Button` — still used on line 250 ✓
- `SelectOptionProps` — still used in the type signature on line 116 and elsewhere ✓
- All other imports remain referenced ✓

## Changes Applied

### Fix 1 — `Button.variant` type changed (Line 250)
**No change needed.** The value `"plain"` is still valid in the new type union (which only added `'stateful'`). The existing code is already compatible.

### Fix 2 — Select family migration / `onSelect` type change (Line 264)
**Applied.** Updated the `onSelect` callback's `value` parameter type from `string | number | undefined` to `SelectOptionProps['value'] | undefined` (line 116) to match the new v6 API signature. The `shouldFocusFirstItemOnOpen` prop (line 267) is still a valid prop on Select in v6, so no change was needed for that sub-incident.

### Fix 3 — Button icon children → `icon` prop (Line 250)
**Applied.** Moved `<TimesIcon aria-hidden />` from Button children to the `icon` prop and made the Button element self-closing. Changed from:
```jsx
<Button variant="plain" onClick={onClearButtonClick} aria-label="Clear input value">
  <TimesIcon aria-hidden />
</Button>
```
to:
```jsx
<Button variant="plain" onClick={onClearButtonClick} aria-label="Clear input value" icon={<TimesIcon aria-hidden />} />
```

### Fix 4 — MenuToggle icon children → `icon` prop (Line 224)
**No change needed.** The MenuToggle has `variant="typeahead"` and contains a `<TextInputGroup>` as children, not an icon element. There is no direct icon child of MenuToggle to migrate.

### Fix 5 — TextInputGroupMain icon children → `icon` prop (Line 233)
**No change needed.** The TextInputGroupMain component has no icon children — it is configured with props only and has no child elements.

### Fix 6 — Select behavioral change (Popper appendTo default) (Line 3)
**No change needed.** This is an advisory/test-related fix about the Popper `appendTo` default changing. The code does not set `popperProps` on the Select component, and no companion test files were listed to update.
